content.problemStatement: Q3) A . Explain the concept of pointer to structure ?   ---3 Marks.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer Age (1 ≤ Age ≤ 100) and the second line contains a string Name (up to 100 characters, no spaces).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line in the exact format: "Age: X, Name: Y" where X is the age and Y is the name.

sampleTestCases.output:
Age: 20, Name: John
Age: 30, Name: Alice
Age: 40, Name: Bob
sampleTestCases.explanation: There are 3 test cases. For each case the program reads the age and the name and prints them in the required format.
A single test case with age 5 and name Zara produces one formatted line.

sampleTestCases.output:
Age: 99, Name: Max
Age: 1, Name: Ada
sampleTestCases.explanation: Two test cases demonstrate handling of both maximum and minimum allowed ages.

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times, each iteration read Age and Name. Store them (or use a pointer/reference to a struct/object) and immediately print them using the required format. No additional processing is needed, so the algorithm runs in linear time with respect to the number of test cases.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the variables used for reading the current test case.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    idx = 1
    out = []
    for _ in range(t):
        age = int(data[idx]); idx += 1
        name = data[idx]; idx += 1
        out.append(f"Age: {age}, Name: {name}")
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
